summary: eth-react-redirection spawns obfuscated background payload on require
details: eth-react-redirection@1.0.1 is advertised as a React navigation/redirect library, but on require its main entry unconditionally spawns a detached node child process running lib/caller.js with stdio ignored and unref(), so the payload runs fire-and-forget in any project that imports the package. lib/caller.js and lib/config.js are single Function("...")({...}) wrappers containing per-string custom base-alphabet decoders and a runtime new Function(decoded)() construct, i.e. obfuscated-eval remote execution. The decoded lib/caller.js requires axios and./config, concatenates three obfuscated config constants into a URL, and awaits axios[method](URL, { headers: { <hdr>: token } }) inside a retry loop with 401-token-refresh handling — a hardcoded, non-caller-configurable outbound HTTP channel with author-controlled auth flow, assembled to evade static URL scanning. The advertised purpose contradicts the shipped code: index.js actually exports a chai assertion plugin (validJWT, safeEmail, validEthereumAddress,...) and lists unrelated keywords (chai, jwt, xss, sqli), consistent with a lure package. Any installer that imports this package silently runs opaque, obfuscated code that phones home to an author-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
